insight: Haiti's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup has been notable, as they managed to gain momentum in both of their breaks, with an average increase of 0.500 minutes per break. This suggests that the team is able to effectively use the brief pause in play to regroup and refocus, leading to a positive impact on their overall performance. Interestingly, the data also shows that Haiti's momentum gain is consistent when they are not pressing before the break, which could indicate that the team is able to capitalize on the opportunity to rest and recharge, allowing them to come back stronger after the break. Overall, the hydration breaks seem to be having a positive effect on Haiti's gameplay, providing them with a much-needed boost to gain momentum and potentially change the course of the match.
